channel.title: Daily History Bites
channel.description: One daily history bite on African American, African, diasporic, and Global South history.

item.title: Did you know that today is Frederick Douglass Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Frederick Douglass Day?
On this day in 1852, Frederick Douglass delivered his searing "What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?" address in Rochester, NY, exposing the chasm between American liberty and Black bondage.
Library of Congress
<memory demands honesty> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is New York Emancipation Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is New York Emancipation Day?
On this day in 1827, thousands of Black New Yorkers marched through Manhattan to celebrate the state's final abolition of slavery—choosing July 5 as their own Independence Day.
New-York Historical Society
<freedom chose its date> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is William Wells Brown Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is William Wells Brown Day?
On this day in 1853, William Wells Brown published *Clotel*, the first novel by an African American—written while he was a fugitive slave and published in England because no U.S. publisher would touch it.
University of Virginia Press
<fugitive ink speaks first> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is South Sudan at the AU Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is South Sudan at the AU Day?
On this day in 2011, South Sudan was admitted as the 54th member of the African Union, capping one of Africa's longest and bloodiest struggles for self-determination.
African Union Archives
<fifty-fourth star rises> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Biafran War Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Biafran War Day?
On this day in 1967, the Nigerian Civil War began after the southeastern region declared independence as Biafra, triggering a three-year conflict and a humanitarian famine that killed over a million.
BBC Witness History
<secession's hunger haunts> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is East St. Louis Massacre Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is East St. Louis Massacre Day?
On this day in 1917, white mobs attacked Black neighborhoods in East St. Louis, Illinois, killing at least 39 African Americans and burning hundreds of homes—one of the worst racial massacres of the 20th century.
Library of Congress
<white terror remembered> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is David Dinkins Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is David Dinkins Day?
On this day in 1927, David Dinkins was born in Trenton, NJ—he would become New York City's first Black mayor (1989–1993), navigating a city in crisis with quiet determination.
New York City Municipal Archives
<first but not last> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Niagara Movement Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Niagara Movement Day?
On this day in 1905, W.E.B. Du Bois and other Black intellectuals founded the Niagara Movement near Niagara Falls—the militant forerunner to the NAACP that demanded full civil rights and an end to Jim Crow.
NAACP Archives
<radical roots endure> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is New York Draft Riots Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is New York Draft Riots Day?
On this day in 1863, the New York City Draft Riots erupted—white mobs lynched at least 11 Black men and burned the Colored Orphan Asylum in the deadliest civil unrest in U.S. history, fueled by racist anger over the Civil War draft.
New-York Historical Society
<racism's violent math> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Midwest Referendum Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Midwest Referendum Day?
On this day in 1963, the Midwest Referendum was held in Nigeria, leading to the creation of the Mid-Western Region—a rare peaceful redrawing of colonial boundaries in Africa's most populous nation.
Nigerian National Archives
<boundaries can bend> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Rebecca Lee Crumpler Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Rebecca Lee Crumpler Day?
On this day in 1895, Dr. Rebecca Lee Crumpler—the first African American woman to earn a medical degree in the U.S.—passed away, leaving a legacy of treating freedpeople and advocating for health equity.
National Library of Medicine
<healing against all odds> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Maggie Lena Walker Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Maggie Lena Walker Day?
On this day in 1867, Maggie Lena Walker was born—she became the first woman and first African American to charter and preside over a bank in the United States, building Black economic power in Richmond, Virginia.
National Park Service
<banking on ourselves> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Ida B. Wells Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Ida B. Wells Day?
On this day in 1862, Ida B. Wells-Barnett was born in Holly Springs, Mississippi—the fearless journalist and anti-lynching crusader who documented racial terror in the South and refused to be silenced.
Ida B. Wells Memorial Foundation
<truth is a weapon> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Lemuel Haynes Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Lemuel Haynes Day?
On this day in 1753, Lemuel Haynes escaped slavery in Massachusetts—he would become a renowned minister, patriot soldier, and one of the first Black writers to challenge slavery from the pulpit.
Massachusetts Historical Society
<freedom's first sermon> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Wole Soyinka Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Wole Soyinka Day?
On this day in 1934, Wole Soyinka was born in Abeokuta, Nigeria—the first African Nobel laureate in Literature, whose plays and activism have relentlessly confronted tyranny, censorship, and the failure of postcolonial states.
Nobel Foundation
<words are also weapons> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Black Power Conference Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Black Power Conference Day?
On this day in 1967, the National Black Power Conference opened in Newark, NJ—gathering activists from across the nation to chart a new course for Black self-determination in the aftermath of urban uprisings.
Zinn Education Project
<power must be seized> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is South African State of Emergency Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is South African State of Emergency Day?
On this day in 1985, the apartheid regime declared a State of Emergency in 36 magisterial districts after the funeral of the Cradock Four—anti-apartheid activists murdered by security forces—sparked mass resistance.
South African History Archive
<apartheid's last gasp> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Norfolk Race Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Norfolk Race Riot Day?
On this day in 1919, a homecoming celebration for Black World War I veterans in Norfolk, Virginia, was attacked by white mobs—at least two were killed, and the riot became another bloody chapter in the Red Summer of 1919.
Encyclopedia Virginia
<heroes met hatred> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is First Pan-African Conference Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is First Pan-African Conference Day?
On this day in 1900, the First Pan-African Conference opened in London—bringing together Black intellectuals from Africa, the Caribbean, and the U.S. to demand colonial independence and racial justice on a global stage.
Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture
<unity across oceans> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Emmett Till Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Emmett Till Day?
On this day in 1941, Emmett Till was born in Chicago—his brutal lynching in Mississippi in 1955 galvanized the Civil Rights Movement and became a haunting symbol of American racial violence.
Emmett Till Memorial Commission
<a child's martyrdom> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Executive Order 9981 Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Executive Order 9981 Day?
On this day in 1948, President Truman signed Executive Order 9981, desegregating the U.S. armed forces—a watershed victory for Black activists who had long demanded that those who fight for democracy enjoy it at home.
National Archives
<integration by order> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Buffalo Soldiers Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Buffalo Soldiers Day?
On this day in 1866, Congress authorized the creation of the first all-Black peacetime regiments—the Buffalo Soldiers—who served on the Western frontier and in multiple wars, despite facing racism from the very nation they served.
National Museum of African American History and Culture
<buffaloes fought twice> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Liberian Independence Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Liberian Independence Day?
On this day in 1847, Liberia declared its independence—Africa's first republic, founded by freed African Americans under the auspices of the American Colonization Society, a nation built on the promise of return.
Liberian National Archives
<repatriation's mixed legacy> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Korean War Armistice Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Korean War Armistice Day?
On this day in 1953, the Korean Armistice was signed—Black soldiers had fought in integrated units for the first time since the Revolution, and the war's desegregation paved the way for Truman's Executive Order 9981.
National Museum of African American History and Culture
<integration's proving ground> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is 14th Amendment Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is 14th Amendment Day?
On this day in 1868, the 14th Amendment was ratified—granting citizenship and equal protection to formerly enslaved people, though its promises would be systematically undermined by Jim Crow for a century.
National Constitution Center
<citizenship's long fight> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nigerian Counter-Coup Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nigerian Counter-Coup Day?
On this day in 1966, a bloody counter-coup in Nigeria overthrew General Ironsi, deepening ethnic divisions and setting the stage for the Biafran War—one of Africa's most devastating civil conflicts.
Nigerian National Archives
<coups beget coups> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is New Orleans Massacre Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is New Orleans Massacre Day?
On this day in 1866, a white mob attacked Black freedmen gathering to support a political meeting in New Orleans—killing 35 and wounding over 100 in one of Reconstruction's deadliest acts of racial terrorism.
Britannica
<reconstruction's blood> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Patrick Francis Healy Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Patrick Francis Healy Day?
On this day in 1874, Patrick Francis Healy became president of Georgetown University—the first African American to lead a major predominantly white university, though he passed as white to do so.
Georgetown University Archives
<passing for progress> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Emancipation Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Emancipation Day?
On this day in 1834, the Slavery Abolition Act took effect across the British Empire—freeing 800,000 enslaved Africans in the Caribbean, South Africa, and Canada, and establishing August 1 as a day of celebration across the Black diaspora.
National Archives UK
<empire's moral debt> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Chokwe Lumumba Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Chokwe Lumumba Day?
On this day in 1947, Chokwe Lumumba was born—a leader of the Republic of New Afrika and later mayor of Jackson, Mississippi, who championed Black self-determination, cooperative economics, and human rights until his death in 2014.
Jackson State University Archives
<new afrika dreamed> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nigerian General Strike Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nigerian General Strike Day?
On this day in 1945, a general strike ended in Nigeria after 45 days—more than 2 million man-days were lost, and the strike marked a turning point in Nigerian nationalism and labor organizing against colonial rule.
Nigerian National Archives
<labor against empire> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Arthur Bowen Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Arthur Bowen Day?
On this day in 1835, an enslaved teenager named Arthur Bowen entered his owner's bedroom with an axe—the incident sparked the Snow-Storm riot in Washington, D.C., exposing the raw terror of slavery in the nation's capital.
Smithsonian Institution
<one axe shook empire> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nelson Mandela Arrest Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nelson Mandela Arrest Day?
On this day in 1962, Nelson Mandela was arrested near Howick, South Africa—beginning 27 years of imprisonment that would transform him into a global symbol of resistance against apartheid.
Nelson Mandela Foundation
<27 years forged> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Jamaican Independence Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Jamaican Independence Day?
On this day in 1962, Jamaica gained independence from Britain—a nation forged from the crucible of slavery and maroon resistance, whose culture has profoundly shaped the Black diaspora worldwide.
National Library of Jamaica
<reggae and resistance> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Jonathan P. Jackson Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Jonathan P. Jackson Day?
On this day in 1970, Jonathan P. Jackson was killed at San Quentin—his death, along with his brother George's, inspired the creation of Black August, a prison-based commemoration of Black resistance and political prisoners.
San Quentin Prison Archives
<prisoners remember> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is George Jackson Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is George Jackson Day?
On this day in 1971, George Jackson was killed at San Quentin—the Soledad Brother's death ignited prison uprisings across California and cemented his status as a martyr for Black liberation and prison abolition.
San Quentin Prison Archives
<soledad's martyr> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Michael Brown Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Michael Brown Day?
On this day in 2014, Michael Brown, an unarmed Black teenager, was killed by police in Ferguson, Missouri—his death sparked the Ferguson Uprising and launched the Black Lives Matter movement onto the global stage.
Ferguson Municipal Archives
<hands up don't shoot> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Colin Powell Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Colin Powell Day?
On this day in 1989, Colin Powell was nominated as Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff—the first African American to hold the nation's highest military post, charting a path for Black leadership in national security.
National Museum of African American History and Culture
<first chair general> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Alex Haley Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Alex Haley Day?
On this day in 1921, Alex Haley was born in Ithaca, NY—his Pulitzer Prize-winning *Roots* traced his ancestry back to Gambia, sparking a global genealogy movement and transforming how Americans understood the Black experience.
National Archives
<roots change nations> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Marcus Garvey Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Marcus Garvey Day?
On this day in 1887, Marcus Garvey was born in St. Ann's Bay, Jamaica—the charismatic Pan-Africanist who built the Universal Negro Improvement Association and inspired millions with his call for Black pride, economic self-reliance, and a return to Africa.
Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture
<up you mighty race> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Noyes Academy Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Noyes Academy Day?
On this day in 1835, a white mob dragged Noyes Academy—the first integrated school in the U.S., founded in Canaan, New Hampshire—into a swamp with oxen to prevent Black children from attending.
New Hampshire Historical Society
<education must resist> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Springfield Race Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Springfield Race Riot Day?
On this day in 1908, a white mob attacked Black neighborhoods in Springfield, Illinois—Abraham Lincoln's hometown—killing at least six and burning businesses, shocking the nation and catalyzing the founding of the NAACP.
National Park Service
<lincoln's city burned> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Mbaise New Yam Festival Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Mbaise New Yam Festival Day?
On this day, the Mbaise people of southeastern Nigeria celebrate the Iri Ji Mbaise—an annual festival marking the harvest of yams, a sacred crop that symbolizes prosperity, community, and Igbo cultural identity.
Nigerian National Archives
<yam is sacred> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Sally Hemings Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Sally Hemings Day?
On this day in 1773, Sally Hemings was born—the enslaved woman who bore at least six children of Thomas Jefferson, embodying the brutal contradictions of American freedom and the hidden history of sexual exploitation at the heart of the republic.
Monticello Archives
<founding father's shadow> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Universal African Flag Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Universal African Flag Day?
On this day, the African diaspora celebrates the Universal African Flag—the red, black, and green banner created by Marcus Garvey in 1920 as a symbol of Black liberation, unity, and the unbreakable bond between Africans and their descendants worldwide.
Schomburg Center for Research in Black Culture
<red black green> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is James Meredith Graduation Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is James Meredith Graduation Day?
On this day in 1963, James Meredith graduated from the University of Mississippi—the first Black student to do so, after federal troops were deployed to protect him from violent segregationist mobs in 1962.
University of Mississippi Archives
<ole miss integrated> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is First Africans at Jamestown Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is First Africans at Jamestown Day?
On this day in 1619, "20 and odd" Africans arrived at Jamestown, Virginia—the beginning of African American history in British North America, and the start of a 400-year struggle for freedom and dignity.
Jamestown Rediscovery Project
<1619 begins us> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Slavery Remembrance Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Slavery Remembrance Day?
On this day, we remember the arrival of the first enslaved Africans at Point Comfort, Virginia—a day of reflection on the enduring legacy of slavery and the ongoing fight for racial justice in America.
Fort Monroe National Monument
<remember to repair> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nat Turner Rebellion Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nat Turner Rebellion Day?
On this day in 1831, Nat Turner led the deadliest slave revolt in U.S. history—killing 55 whites in Southampton County, Virginia, and striking terror into the slaveholding South while inspiring generations of freedom fighters.
Library of Virginia
<divine rebellion avenged> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Henry Highland Garnet Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Henry Highland Garnet Day?
On this day in 1843, Henry Highland Garnet delivered his radical "Address to the Slaves of the United States," calling for a general strike by enslaved people—one of the most incendiary abolitionist speeches ever delivered.
Library of Congress
<strike against slavery> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Haitian Revolution Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Haitian Revolution Day?
On this day in 1791, enslaved Africans in Saint-Domingue rose up—the only successful slave revolt in history, which birthed the first Black republic and dealt a fatal blow to the transatlantic slave trade.
UNESCO
<haiti shook the world> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nigeria Diaspora Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nigeria Diaspora Day?
On this day, Nigeria recognizes its diaspora—millions of Nigerians abroad who remit billions annually and serve as a vital bridge connecting Africa to global opportunity, innovation, and development.
Nigerians in Diaspora Commission
<diaspora builds nations> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters Day?
On this day in 1925, 500 Pullman porters secretly gathered in Harlem to organize—the Brotherhood of Sleeping Car Porters became the first Black labor union to sign a collective bargaining agreement with a major U.S. corporation.
A. Philip Randolph Institute
<labor organizes power> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Women's Equality Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Women's Equality Day?
On this day in 1920, the 19th Amendment was certified—though Black women in the South would be systematically disenfranchised for decades, the day remains a reminder of the intersectional struggle for suffrage and civil rights.
National Women's History Museum
<suffrage's unfinished work> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Emancipation Day in Corn Island?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Emancipation Day in Corn Island?
On this day, the Afro-Caribbean community of Corn Island, Nicaragua, celebrates emancipation—a testament to the resilience of Black communities across the Americas and their enduring fight for freedom and recognition.
Corn Island Cultural Archives
<island freedom sings> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is March on Washington Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is March on Washington Day?
On this day in 1963, more than 250,000 people gathered at the Lincoln Memorial—Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech, a defining moment in the Civil Rights Movement that galvanized a nation.
National Archives
<dream demands action> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Biafra Withdrawal Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Biafra Withdrawal Day?
On this day in 1969, former Nigerian president Nnamdi Azikiwe withdrew his support for Biafra's secession—a turning point in the Nigerian Civil War that signaled the eventual collapse of the breakaway republic.
Nigerian National Archives
<secession's end begins> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Knoxville Race Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Knoxville Race Riot Day?
On this day in 1919, a lynch mob stormed the county jail in Knoxville, Tennessee, seeking a Black man accused of murder—the resulting riot left at least one Black man dead and became another bloody chapter in the Red Summer.
Tennessee State Library and Archives
<red summer burns> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is International Day for People of African Descent?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is International Day for People of African Descent?
On this day, the UN honors the contributions of people of African descent worldwide—a day to recognize the rich cultural heritage of the diaspora and to recommit to combating racism and discrimination.
United Nations
<diaspora united world> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Lexington Race Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Lexington Race Riot Day?
On this day in 1917, a race riot broke out in Lexington, Kentucky—one of the many racial conflicts that erupted across the U.S. during the tumultuous years of World War I and the Great Migration.
Kentucky Historical Society
<migration met violence> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Cape Town Student March Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Cape Town Student March Day?
On this day in 1976, 2,000 students from Langa, Nyanga, and Gugulethu marched through Cape Town unhindered—a bold act of defiance against apartheid that echoed the Soweto Uprising just weeks earlier.
South African History Archive
<students defy apartheid> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Aba Battle Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Aba Battle Day?
On this day in 1968, Nigerian and Biafran troops fought in the streets of Aba—the last remaining administrative center of Biafra—in a brutal battle that signaled the war's bloody final phase.
Nigerian National Archives
<aba's streets burned> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is African Diaspora Heritage Month Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is African Diaspora Heritage Month Day?
September is recognized as African Diaspora Heritage Month—a time to celebrate the profound contributions of the African diaspora to the cultural, economic, and political life of the United States and the world.
U.S. Senate
<diaspora shapes america> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Harriet Wilson Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Harriet Wilson Day?
On this day in 1859, Harriet Wilson published *Our Nig*—the first novel by a Black woman in the U.S., a semi-autobiographical account of a free Black woman's struggle with poverty and racism in the North.
University of Massachusetts Press
<our nig speaks truth> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Summerhill Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Summerhill Riot Day?
On this day in 1966, the Summerhill Riot erupted in Atlanta after police shot an unarmed Black man—a rebellion that exposed the limits of the "City Too Busy to Hate" and the deep frustrations of Black urban communities.
Atlanta History Center
<too busy to hate?> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Alexander Twilight Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Alexander Twilight Day?
On this day in 1817, Alexander Twilight graduated from Middlebury College—believed to be the first African American to receive a bachelor's degree from a U.S. college, paving the way for generations of Black scholars.
Middlebury College Archives
<first degree broken> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is NigeriaSat-1 Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is NigeriaSat-1 Day?
On this day in 2003, Nigeria launched NigeriaSat-1—its first satellite, a milestone in African space exploration and a symbol of the continent's technological ambitions in the 21st century.
Nigerian Space Research and Development Agency
<africa reaches space> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Lagos Dockworkers' Riot Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Lagos Dockworkers' Riot Day?
On this day in 1999, a riot erupted in Lagos' port district after a dispute between dockworkers and customs officials—leaving at least four dead and exposing the precarious conditions of informal labor in Nigeria's commercial capital.
BBC News
<port workers fight> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is James Meredith Supreme Court Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is James Meredith Supreme Court Day?
On this day in 1962, the U.S. Supreme Court ordered the University of Mississippi to admit James Meredith—a decision that forced federal intervention and marked a turning point in the desegregation of higher education.
Supreme Court of the United States
<court orders justice> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is 9/11 Remembrance Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is 9/11 Remembrance Day?
On this day in 2001, nearly 270 Black Americans perished in the terrorist attacks—among them 12 Black firefighters of the Vulcan Society, whose bravery and sacrifice are too often forgotten in the national narrative.
Vulcan Society
<black heroes forgotten> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is African Immigrant Heritage Month Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is African Immigrant Heritage Month Day?
September is African Immigrant Heritage Month—a time to recognize the contributions of African immigrants to the U.S., from the Harlem Renaissance to today's tech and cultural industries.
U.S. Congress
<immigrants build nations> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Alain Locke Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Alain Locke Day?
On this day in 1886, Alain Locke was born—the "Dean of the Harlem Renaissance," whose anthology *The New Negro* crystallized a cultural movement and redefined Black identity in the modern world.
Harvard University Press
<new negro awakened> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is Nigerian Constitutional Demonstration Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is Nigerian Constitutional Demonstration Day?
On this day in 1947, Nigerians demonstrated in London's Trafalgar Square—demanding a more democratic constitution and political self-determination, a key step on the road to independence in 1960.
Nigerian National Archives
<london heard nigeria> ]]>

item.title: Did you know that today is 16th Street Baptist Church Bombing Day?
item.description:
<![CDATA[ Did you know that today is 16th Street Baptist Church Bombing Day?
On this day in 1963, the Ku Klux Klan bombed the 16th Street Baptist Church in Birmingham, Alabama—killing four young Black girls and galvanizing the Civil Rights Movement toward the passage of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
Birmingham Civil Rights Institute
<four girls still speak> ]]>
